result.stdout:
If you are not using this CLI as a dotnet tool, remember to substitute "fskaggle" with the name of your executable.

EXAMPLES:

    Download the entire dataset from https://www.kaggle.com/dataset-owner/dataset-name :
        fskaggle dataset-owner dataset-name

    Only download the file file.csv from within the dataset:
        fskaggle dataset-owner dataset-name -f file.csv

    Download file in ./Data:
        fskaggle dataset-owner dataset-name -f file.csv -o Data

    Same, but with the kaggle API token in a kaggle.json file within the same directory and also overwrite any existing files:
        fskaggle dataset-owner dataset-name -f file.csv -o Data -c kaggle.json --overwrite

USAGE: FsKaggle.CLI [--help] [--credentials <string>] [--output <string>]
                    [--file <string>] [--overwrite] [--whatif] [--examples]
                    [<Owner> <Name>]

DATASET:

    <Owner> <Name>        The name of the dataset and the kaggle user under
                          whom it is published.

OPTIONS:

    --credentials, -c <string>
                          Path to credentials file, normally kaggle.json.
                          Defaults to ~/.kaggle/kaggle.json
    --output, -o <string> The folder to download the dataset in. If missing
                          we'll attempt to create it. Default is current folder.
    --file, -f <string>   Download a particular file from the dataset. If
                          ommited the entire dataset will be downloaded as a
                          zip.
    --overwrite           Overwrite already existing files.
    --whatif              Validate all arguements but don't actually query
                          kaggle.
    --examples, -x, -ex   Show usage examples.
    --help                display this list of options.
